taskCaption: drawer goes from near-closed to RLBench-calibrated open (articulation-event diagnostic)

pilotMetadata.tests: drawer articulation goes from near-zero (0.0) to the RLBench-calibrated extension (0.234 m): one hard ARTICULATION_GOAL (goal_satisfaction), one ARTICULATION_CHANGE event (event_presence), and the 0.0->0.234 articulation transition (articulation_transitions). One step beyond the value-only target: it asserts the drawer STARTED closed and CHANGED, not only its terminal value.
pilotMetadata.deferredNote: Deliberately NO contact, handle-contact, CONTACT_BEGIN event, temporal edge, or strict contact-before-motion order. The single ARTICULATION_CHANGE event carries event_presence + articulation_transitions support but ZERO event-order pair support (there is no second event to order against), so event_order stays vacuous. The adapter has no honest RLBench evidence for who/what caused the motion; asserting contact or order would be unevidenced. Those remain a later target (2F-3+).
pilotMetadata.source: RLBench OpenDrawer (bottom/middle/top), live Runpod rerun 2026-06-14; terminal extension min 0.2347 / mean 0.2356 / max 0.2362 m across 9 fresh demos. See pilots/rlbench/fixtures/live_runpod_20260614_rerun/.
pilotMetadata.toleranceNote: Intended/documentary only and NOT read by the verifier. The FROZEN matcher enforces a single global MatcherConfig.articulation_tol (0.05 m), not this per-target field; the initial 0.0 and terminal 0.234 are both well clear of the 0.05 m window so the transition registers and the goal is met. csg.canon ignores unknown top-level keys, so this block never enters any probe.
pilotMetadata.notAGoldTask: Pilot diagnostic, deliberately NOT added to gold_tests/. It asserts the drawer started near-closed, underwent an articulation change, and reached the calibrated extension; it does NOT assert the agent contacted or caused the motion. Read it for articulation-event mappability, not as a full open_drawer acceptance test.
